summary: Excellent audience recording captures a fiery Shakedown performance with smoking Brent on keys, Jerry’s soaring guitar on M&MU into Big River, and solid versions of Black Peter and Nobody's Fault But Mine.
review: Fans rave about this bootleg as one of the best‑heard Shakedown shows, highlighting blazing keyboards from Brent and a scorching guitar run by Jerry that carries Mississippi Half‑Step Uptown Toodeloo into Big River. The audience (AUD) source is praised for its clarity and is considered the top version among the five existing tapes, making it both a great listening experience and a memorable snapshot of the Barton Hall atmosphere.

ai_rating.rationale: All five reviews are strongly positive (average 4.4 stars) and consistently commend both the musical performance and the high‑quality audience recording, giving confidence in a rating near the top of the scale.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Clear, crisp AUD capture; described as the best sounding version among multiple tapes and noted as an "excellent quality AUD" by reviewers.

show_quality.setlist_flow: Solid first set followed by an awesome second set; overall pacing praised and the concert is remembered as a defining Shakedown performance.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Garcia’s guitar was described as "smokin'" on M&MU into Big River.
band_member_comments.Keys: Brent's keyboard work was called "just smokin'" throughout the show.
